response: Photosynthesis is a process plants perform to produce sustenance. Sunlight powers this process, providing energy. Plants possess structures called chloroplasts, where photosynthesis primarily occurs. Sunlight strikes chlorophyll, a pigment in chloroplasts, sparking energy absorption. This energy splits water (H₂O) sourced from soil, releasing oxygen (O₂) as a byproduct. Simultaneously, plants pull carbon dioxide (CO₂) from the surrounding air. Using sunlight-powered energy, plants synthesize glucose (C₆H₁₂O₆), a sugar serving as sustenance. This synthesis supports plant growth and sustains ecosystems. Photosynthesis plays a pivotal role in sustaining life on Earth, supplying oxygen and forming the foundation of food chains.
